data.q: In Rust, what does the ownership system primarily help with?
o: Memory safety without a garbage collector
data.e: Rust's ownership system ensures memory safety and prevents data races without needing a garbage collector.

data.q: In Rust, which keyword is used to create a reference to a value without taking ownership?
data.e: The '&' symbol is used to create an immutable reference to a value without taking ownership.

data.q: What happens if you try to have two mutable references to the same data simultaneously in Rust?
o: Compiler error due to borrowing rules
data.e: Rust's borrowing rules prevent having two mutable references to the same data at the same time to ensure safety.

data.q: What does the term 'borrowing' mean in Rust?
o: Creating a reference to a value without taking ownership
Copying the data to a new variable
data.e: Borrowing means creating references (& or &mut) to data without transferring ownership.

data.q: Which of these can be mutated in Rust?
o: The original data if only borrowed immutably
data.e: Only mutable references (&mut T) allow mutation. Immutable references (&T) do not permit mutation.

data.q: What does the 'move' keyword do in Rust closures?
o: Moves ownership of captured variables into the closure
data.e: 'move' forces the closure to take ownership of captured variables instead of borrowing them.

data.q: Which trait must a type implement to be safely copied rather than moved in Rust?
data.e: 'Copy' types implement implicit copying instead of moving, usually for simple scalar types.

data.q: What is the lifetime specifier used for in Rust?
o: Describing scope of references to ensure validity
data.e: Lifetimes help the compiler check that references remain valid and prevent dangling references.

data.q: What does borrowing with '&mut' mean in Rust?
data.e: '&mut' is a mutable reference that allows modifying the borrowed data.

data.q: Why can Rust prevent data races at compile time?
o: Because of ownership and borrowing rules
data.e: Rust's ownership and borrowing rules enforced at compile time eliminate data races.
